mission.title: Install and Configure Inspektor Gadget on Kubernetes
mission.description: Inspektor Gadget is a set of tools and framework for data collection and system inspection on Kubernetes clusters and Linux hosts using eBPF. Installing it allows you to leverage eBPF for enhanced observability and monitoring of your applications and infrastructure.

steps.title: Add the Inspektor Gadget Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm repository for Inspektor Gadget to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add inspektor-gadget https://inspektor-gadget.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Inspektor Gadget using Helm
steps.description:
Install Inspektor Gadget in the `inspektor-gadget` namespace. This command will also create the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget --create-namespace --version v0.49.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Inspektor Gadget pods are running successfully in the `inspektor-gadget` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Inspektor Gadget deployment. Edit the deployment to set resource limits as needed.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment inspektor-gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget
```

steps.title: Access the Inspektor Gadget dashboard
steps.description:
If there is a dashboard or service to access, set up port forwarding to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/inspektor-gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Inspektor Gadget pods in the `Running` state when you run `kubectl get pods`. You can also access the dashboard through the port-forwarding command provided.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add inspektor-gadget https://inspektor-gadget.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace inspektor-gadget --create-namespace --version v0.49.1

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
